positive_reviews: ACT mouthwash tastes very minty and fresh without being sickly sweet. It's a good balance. I've recently incorporated liquid fluoride into my dental care routine, and the difference it has made is remarkable. Initially, I wasn't very familiar with liquid fluoride, but after my dentist suggested it as an additional measure to protect my teeth, I decided to give it a shot. One of the most significant benefits I've noticed is the enhanced protection against cavities. Despite regular brushing and flossing, I used to get the occasional cavity, which was frustrating. However, since starting with liquid fluoride, I haven't had a single new cavity. It feels like an added shield for my teeth. The application process is straightforward. I use it once a day, usually at night after brushing and flossing. The liquid fluoride I use comes with a small measuring cup, making it easy to pour out the correct amount. I swish it around in my mouth for about a minute and then spit it out. There's no rinsing afterward, which means the fluoride can work its magic overnight. Another positive aspect is the reduction in tooth sensitivity. I occasionally experienced discomfort when eating very hot or cold foods, but this has significantly decreased since I started using liquid fluoride. It seems to be strengthening my enamel and making my teeth more resilient. Overall, I'm thrilled with the results of using liquid fluoride. It has become an essential part of my dental care routine, and I feel more confident about the health of my teeth. If you're looking for an effective way to boost your oral hygiene and protect against cavities, I highly recommend giving liquid fluoride a try. Based on my experience, it's a simple yet powerful addition that delivers noticeable results.

positive_reviews: It'd been a long time since my wife and I had used mouth wash, but our dentist recommended it and gave specifics about the bottle needing to have "Fluoride" or "Anticavity" in the title, so it worked out that this ACT product had both. We bought the six bottles for $32, which isn't the best bargain I've had on Amazon, but I figured they would last a while and would not go bad. We have used it for at least a week now, and I must say there are at least three great things about it: 1. The bottle design is really innovative. You squeeze the bottle and liquid squirts out into the top from two little spigots and then sits there for you to pour into your mouth from the bottle. I'm probably not describing it well, but it's smart, simple, and effective. It saves the need to pour into a cap or a separate cup. There is also a fill line drawn near the top of the bottle, so you just squeeze until the liquid hits that mark. 2. There is no alcohol in the product so the burn factor is limited. I remember mouth wash being kind of painful. This still burns a little, but not nearly as much as the traditional product. 3. Great taste. It's hard to go wrong with mint, but it's still something to be appreciated. Good stuff!

My dentist recommended a fluoride mouthwash to help rebuild enamel on a tooth with a sensitive root. I had been using an alcohol-based mouthwash which did a great job helping keep plaque under control and made my teeth feel really clean. Unfortunately, I gave it up due to concerns raised by recent studies as far as links to oral cancer. So, I switched to ACT, a non-alcohol mouthwash. It seems to work well and has a really clever built-in measuring system. It doesn't leave my teeth feeling quite as clean but the nerve sensitivity in my tooth has gone away...I'm assuming (but have no proof) that it is due to the fluoride in the ACT. It's worth noting that, despite the fact that my local water system uses fluoride, we have a lot of minerals in the water that give it an off taste so I drink and cook with water processed using reverse osmosis which removes the fluoride (among many other things). So, I've gone over 20 years without any regular fluoride exposure that the ACT helps correct.

We've used ACT Rinse for years, at our dentist's suggestion. Right before bed, after brushing, swish a mouthful around for a minute and spit - you'll be coating your clean teeth in fluoride protection. It helps protect against cavities by restoring the enamel on your teeth. It has a mild minty flavor, not nearly as strong as Scope, and also come in Bubble Gum flavor for kids. There is a slight saccharine aftertaste but nothing too unpleasant - especially for the protection this product gives. We have un-fluoridated well water at our house, so adding the extra fluoride protection is important for our kids' developing teeth. So far none of our kids has ever had a cavity, so I'm crediting our success to good brushing habits, limiting sodas and sugary foods, and ACT Rinse. With Amazon's Super Saver shipping, this 6-pack is a great buy.
